title: Heiseria simsioides
intro: Heiseria simsioides, also known as the Siam tea tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its leaves have been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving headaches, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Heiseria simsioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the leaves of Heiseria simsioides are used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
